headline: Super Micro Stock Surges 20% After It Says Gross Margins Will Nearly Double
summary: Shares of  Super Micro  surged more than 20% in after-hours trading Tuesday after the artificial intelligence server firm said that its gross margins for the quarter ending in June will be in the range of 15% to 17%—double the 8.2% to 8.4% from its prior guidance.  While revenue is on track to be near the lower end of prior guidance of $11 billion to $12.5 billion, Super Micro’s backlog rose to record levels in the fourth quarter.  In March, the Department of Justice charged the company’s co-founder Yih-Shyan “Wally” Liaw over an alleged plan to smuggle U.S.-assembled servers to China.

content.title: Super Micro Stock Surges 20% After It Says Gross Margins Will Nearly Double
content.summary: Shares of  Super Micro  surged more than 20% in after-hours trading Tuesday after the artificial intelligence server firm said that its gross margins for the quarter ending in June will be in the range of 15% to 17%—double the 8.2% to 8.4% from its prior guidance.  While revenue is on track to be near the lower end of prior guidance of $11 billion to $12.5 billion, Super Micro’s backlog rose to record levels in the fourth quarter.  In March, the Department of Justice charged the company’s co-founder Yih-Shyan “Wally” Liaw over an alleged plan to smuggle U.S.-assembled servers to China.

headline: Supermicro shares jump as margin outlook and record backlog impress investors (NASDAQ:SMCI)
summary: Preliminary results drive strong premarket gainsShares of Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) climbed around 15% in premarket trading on Wednesday after the company released preliminary fourth-quarter figures highlighting significantly stronger profitability and record customer demand. The stock initially rallied about 20% in after-hours trading following the announcement before giving back part of those gains as several analysts urged investors to remain cautious.

headline: At Last, Some Good News for Super Micro Stock
summary: Super Micro Computer  stock surged in premarket trading after the artificial-intelligence server firm released preliminary results for the quarter ending in June.  The highlight was Super Micro’s gross margins for the quarter ending in June will be in the range of 15% to 17%—double the 8.2% to 8.4% it had previously expected.  The margin news was good enough to cause investors to look past Super Micro’s revenue guidance, which is on track to be near the lower end of prior guidance of $11 billion to $12.5 billion.

headline: Oil prices rise another 3%, while Wall Street drifts in mixed trading
summary: The U.S. stock market is drifting in mixed trading Wednesday, while oil prices climb another 3% as fighting continues in the war with Iran.  Philip Morris International rose 3% after the seller of Marlboro cigarettes reported stronger profit and revenue for the latest quarter than expected.  CEO John Stankey also said the telecom is accelerating plans to send roughly $10 billion to its shareholders this year through the buybacks of its stock.

content.title: Oil prices rise another 3%, while Wall Street drifts in mixed trading
content.summary: The U.S. stock market is drifting in mixed trading Wednesday, while oil prices climb another 3% as fighting continues in the war with Iran.  Philip Morris International rose 3% after the seller of Marlboro cigarettes reported stronger profit and revenue for the latest quarter than expected.  CEO John Stankey also said the telecom is accelerating plans to send roughly $10 billion to its shareholders this year through the buybacks of its stock.

headline: Super Micro (SMCI) Climbs 27% as Orders Surge to $60B; More Hedge Funds Buy In
summary: Super Micro Computer Inc. (NASDAQ:SMCI) surged by as much as 26.6 percent in intra-day trading on Wednesday to $32.28 apiece as investors placed bets on the company’s growth prospects after receiving more than $60 billion in order backlogs in the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2026. The stock closed 19.84 percent higher at $30.56 apiece. […]

headline: Is Dell Technologies (DELL) Undervalued On AI Server Volatility?
summary: Dell Technologies (DELL) is back in focus after its stock moved sharply alongside Super Micro Computer and Hewlett Packard Enterprise, as traders treated the AI server peers as a single trade tied to sector wide demand. See our latest analysis for Dell Technologies. Dell Technologies has seen sharp swings around AI server headlines, with a 1 day share price return of 5.83% but a 7 day share price return that fell 11.67%. This comes alongside a much stronger 90 day share price return of 88.28%...
